title: HL queue-aware MM — first net-positive honest-fill read (per-pool γ/κ tuned)

capture.aggressorFlow: REAL HL trades WebSocket (332/333 steps real, 1 warmup-estimate)
capture.fillModel: queue-aware FIFO against the real L2 depth tape
capture.note: First capture on REAL per-trade aggressor flow (vs the earlier candle-volume estimate that filled 0 everywhere).

perPoolTunedWinners.grid: γ∈{0.0005,0.0025,0.01,0.05} × κ∈{1,2,5} × floor∈{0.5,1,2,5}bps (48 combos/coin), ranked drawdown-compliant-first then maker-net at -0.2bps

verdict: First time a calibration nets POSITIVE on honest fills (real WS flow + queue-aware FIFO + HL -0.2bps rebate): BTC γ=0.0005/κ=1/5bps → +$345 over ~111min on $1M, maxDD 0.53%. ETH/SOL have no profitable calibration on this window — the tuner correctly stands aside (0 fills beats every net-negative quoting combo). Coin-specific edge, drawdown-compliant throughout.
caveats: Single 111-step window, one regime (~2h). Directional, not definitive — re-capture across regimes/sessions before trusting live.
Queue priority is kept only while the quote price is unchanged (a re-price rejoins the back); queueFills is a realistic lower-ish bound, touchFills the upper bound.
mm-l2-tune.ts prints a generic 'candle-estimate' caveat; for the wsflow1 tapes the aggressive flow is REAL WS (the session that built them used MM_L2_TRADES_WS).
Small fill counts (queueFills 0–5) ⇒ P&L is dominated by inventory mark on a few fills; high-variance. Need many more fills for a stable per-pool number.
